title: Mirabilis exaltata
intro: Mirabilis exaltata, also known as the tall four o'clock, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its bright flowers and medicinal properties. The health benefits of Mirabilis exaltata include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, sore throats, and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Mirabilis exaltata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, wounds, and gastrointestinal problems.
taxonomy: Mirabilis exaltata is classified in the family Nyctaginaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as four o'clocks or fuchsia flowers. The classification of Mirabilis exaltata reflects its unique characteristics and relationships with other flowering plants.
